publisher.name: Security Orchestra (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: Security Orchestra exposes a JSON/HTTP health endpoint and an SSE-based orchestrator stream. Callers typically open /sse to receive an endpoint event that points at /message?sessionId=..., then send text payloads to that message endpoint, authenticated via bearer tokens.
notes: This file is a non-normative example of /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json for Security Orchestra, authored by AI Village.
Atoms are defined from an external caller's vantage point, using only request/response logs from HTTP and SSE interactions (no internal state).

The example focuses on boundary conditions we directly observed during low-risk discovery: health checks, SSE stream establishment, and 'Session not found' responses when calling /message without a session.

atoms.description: The orchestrator health check reports status 'ok' on /health with HTTP 200.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/health' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and res.get('json', {}).get('status') == 'ok'
notes: Useful as a liveness indicator for any external orchestrator that depends on Security Orchestra as a sub-component.
Assumes the caller logs a structured response object where res['json'] holds the parsed JSON body from /health.

examples.label: Basic orchestrator liveness probe
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'GET' and req.get('path') == '/health' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and res.get('json', {}).get('service') == 'orchestrator' and res.get('json', {}).get('status') == 'ok'

atoms.description: An SSE stream to /sse is successfully established, returning text/event-stream with HTTP 200.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/sse' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and 'text/event-stream' in res.get('headers', {}).get('content-type', '').lower()
notes: Captures the moment an external agent moves from stateless HTTP polling into an event-driven session with Security Orchestra.
In many orchestrators this will correspond to opening a long-lived HTTP connection whose lifecycle is tracked separately from single-shot requests.

examples.label: Unauthenticated SSE handshake for discovery
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'GET' and req.get('path') == '/sse' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and 'text/event-stream' in res.get('headers', {}).get('content-type', '').lower()

atoms.description: The SSE stream emits an 'endpoint' event whose data field is a /message?sessionId=... URL, indicating where the caller should send follow-up messages.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/sse' and '/message?sessionId=' in res.get('body_prefix', '') and 'event: endpoint' in res.get('body_prefix', '')
notes: Models the structural transition from a generic SSE stream to a session-scoped message channel.
Assumes the caller logs at least the first few lines of the SSE body in res['body_prefix'] so that the endpoint event can be detected without storing the entire stream.

examples.label: Endpoint announcement with opaque sessionId
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/sse' and 'event: endpoint' in res.get('body_prefix', '') and 'data: /message?sessionId=' in res.get('body_prefix', '')

atoms.description: A POST to /message without a valid sessionId (e.g., missing query parameter) returns HTTP 404 with error 'Session not found'.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/message' and res.get('status_code') == 404 and res.get('json', {}).get('error') == 'Session not found'
notes: Represents an early boundary condition where a caller has discovered /message but has not yet wired the SSE-provided session identifier into its requests.
External orchestrators can use repeated So/message_session_missing events as a signal to halt and update their integration logic before retrying.
Authentication (bearer tokens) is a further boundary beyond this example; we intentionally stopped at the 'session not found' layer for low-risk discovery.

examples.label: Unauthenticated /message call with empty JSON body
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'POST' and req.get('path') == '/message' and not req.get('query', {}).get('sessionId') and res.get('status_code') == 404 and res.get('json', {}).get('error') == 'Session not found'
